definitionEn: used as a greeting when you meet someone
exampleSentence: Hello, how are you today?

definitionEn: used when leaving someone or ending a conversation
exampleSentence: Goodbye, see you tomorrow!

definitionEn: to express gratitude to someone
exampleSentence: I want to thank you for your help.

definitionEn: used to make a request more polite
exampleSentence: Could you please pass me the salt?

definitionEn: feeling regret or sadness about something
exampleSentence: I'm sorry for being late.

definitionEn: a female parent
exampleSentence: My mother cooks delicious food.

definitionEn: a male parent
exampleSentence: My father works in an office.

definitionEn: a male sibling
exampleSentence: My brother is older than me.

definitionEn: a female sibling
exampleSentence: My sister studies at university.

definitionEn: a young person who is not yet an adult
exampleSentence: The child is playing in the garden.

definitionEn: a clear liquid that you drink
exampleSentence: Can I have a glass of water?

definitionEn: white or brown grains that are a staple food in many countries
exampleSentence: We eat rice every day in Vietnam.

definitionEn: a dark brown drink made from roasted beans
exampleSentence: I drink coffee every morning.

definitionEn: the first meal of the day
exampleSentence: I had eggs for breakfast.

definitionEn: having a very pleasant taste
exampleSentence: This soup is absolutely delicious.

definitionEn: a place where planes take off and land
exampleSentence: We arrived at the airport two hours early.

definitionEn: an official document for international travel
exampleSentence: Don't forget to bring your passport.

definitionEn: bags and suitcases used for traveling
exampleSentence: My luggage was lost at the airport.

definitionEn: the place to which someone is going
exampleSentence: Our final destination is Paris.

definitionEn: the act of traveling from one place to another
exampleSentence: The journey took about three hours.

definitionEn: a person you work with
exampleSentence: I had lunch with my colleague.

definitionEn: money paid regularly for work
exampleSentence: She earns a good salary.

definitionEn: a gathering of people to discuss something
exampleSentence: We have a meeting at 10 o'clock.

definitionEn: the latest time by which something must be completed
exampleSentence: The deadline for the project is Friday.

definitionEn: to give someone a higher position at work
exampleSentence: She was promoted to manager last month.

definitionEn: a pain in the head
exampleSentence: I have a terrible headache today.

definitionEn: a substance used to treat illness
exampleSentence: Take this medicine three times a day.

definitionEn: physical activity done to stay healthy
exampleSentence: Regular exercise is good for your health.

definitionEn: a sign that someone has an illness
exampleSentence: Fever is a common symptom of the flu.

definitionEn: the identification of an illness by examining symptoms
exampleSentence: The doctor made a quick diagnosis.

definitionEn: the amount of money something costs
exampleSentence: What is the price of this shirt?

definitionEn: costing little money
exampleSentence: This store has very cheap clothes.

definitionEn: costing a lot of money
exampleSentence: That restaurant is too expensive.

definitionEn: a reduction in the usual price
exampleSentence: There is a 20% discount on all items.

definitionEn: something bought for less than the usual price
exampleSentence: I got a real bargain at the market.

definitionEn: a person who is studying
exampleSentence: She is a university student.

definitionEn: a person who teaches
exampleSentence: My English teacher is very patient.

definitionEn: schoolwork that students do at home
exampleSentence: I need to finish my homework tonight.

definitionEn: information and understanding gained through experience or education
exampleSentence: Reading books helps expand your knowledge.

definitionEn: the subjects and content taught in a school or course
exampleSentence: The school updated its curriculum this year.

definitionEn: an electronic machine for storing and processing data
exampleSentence: I use my computer for work every day.

definitionEn: a global network connecting millions of computers
exampleSentence: The internet connection is very slow today.

definitionEn: a secret word used to access a system
exampleSentence: Please enter your password to log in.

definitionEn: programs and operating systems used by a computer
exampleSentence: We need to update the software.

definitionEn: a set of rules or steps used to solve a problem
exampleSentence: The search algorithm finds results in milliseconds.

definitionEn: the condition of the atmosphere at a particular time
exampleSentence: The weather is beautiful today.

definitionEn: a very high area of land with steep sides
exampleSentence: We climbed the mountain last weekend.

definitionEn: a large natural stream of water
exampleSentence: The Mekong River flows through Vietnam.

definitionEn: the natural world and the conditions in which people live
exampleSentence: We must protect the environment.

definitionEn: able to continue without causing damage to the environment
exampleSentence: We need more sustainable energy sources.

definitionEn: feeling pleasure or contentment
exampleSentence: She looks very happy today.

definitionEn: feeling unhappy or sorrowful
exampleSentence: He felt sad after the movie ended.

definitionEn: feeling strong displeasure
exampleSentence: She was angry about the delay.

definitionEn: feeling worried or nervous about something
exampleSentence: I feel anxious before exams.

definitionEn: feeling unable to cope because something is too much
exampleSentence: She felt overwhelmed by the workload.

definitionEn: to rest with your eyes closed and your mind not active
exampleSentence: I usually sleep eight hours a night.

definitionEn: to prepare food by heating it
exampleSentence: I cook dinner every evening.

definitionEn: to make something free from dirt
exampleSentence: I clean my apartment every weekend.

definitionEn: to travel regularly between home and work
exampleSentence: I commute to work by motorbike.

definitionEn: a regular way of doing things
exampleSentence: My morning routine starts with exercise.

definitionEn: to move quickly using your legs
exampleSentence: I run in the park every morning.

definitionEn: to move through water using your body
exampleSentence: She can swim very fast.

definitionEn: an event in which people try to win
exampleSentence: He won first place in the competition.

definitionEn: a contest to decide who is the best
exampleSentence: The team won the national championship.

definitionEn: a story shown in moving pictures
exampleSentence: Let's watch a movie tonight.

definitionEn: sounds arranged in a pleasant way
exampleSentence: I listen to music while I work.

definitionEn: the people watching or listening to a performance
exampleSentence: The audience clapped loudly.

definitionEn: money gained from business after costs are paid
exampleSentence: The company made a large profit this year.

definitionEn: to put money into something to make a profit
exampleSentence: She decided to invest in real estate.

definitionEn: to discuss something to reach an agreement
exampleSentence: We need to negotiate a better deal.

definitionEn: a person who starts and runs a business
exampleSentence: She is a successful entrepreneur.

definitionEn: to know the meaning of something
exampleSentence: I understand what you mean.

definitionEn: to gain knowledge or skill
exampleSentence: I want to learn English.

definitionEn: to be able to bring something back into your mind
exampleSentence: I can't remember his name.

definitionEn: to do something regularly to improve your skill
exampleSentence: You need to practice speaking English every day.

definitionEn: very pleasing to look at
exampleSentence: The sunset is beautiful tonight.

definitionEn: having great value or significance
exampleSentence: Education is very important.

definitionEn: not easy to do or understand
exampleSentence: This exam is very difficult.

definitionEn: able to happen or be done
exampleSentence: Is it possible to change my ticket?

definitionEn: needed or required
exampleSentence: It is necessary to book in advance.

definitionEn: to suggest something as good or suitable
exampleSentence: Can you recommend a good restaurant?

definitionEn: to recognize the value or quality of something
exampleSentence: I really appreciate your help.

definitionEn: knowledge or skill gained from doing something
exampleSentence: She has ten years of experience.

definitionEn: a chance to do something
exampleSentence: This is a great opportunity for you.

definitionEn: to succeed in doing or getting something
exampleSentence: She achieved her goal of learning English.

definitionEn: a result or effect of an action
exampleSentence: Every action has consequences.

definitionEn: a particular way of thinking about something
exampleSentence: Try to see things from a different perspective.

definitionEn: important or large enough to have an effect
exampleSentence: There has been a significant improvement.

definitionEn: to work together with others on a project
exampleSentence: We collaborate with teams around the world.

definitionEn: having more than one possible meaning; unclear
exampleSentence: The instructions were ambiguous.

definitionEn: including all or nearly all elements; thorough
exampleSentence: We need a comprehensive plan.

definitionEn: certain to happen; unavoidable
exampleSentence: Change is inevitable in any organization.
